skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guides collaborative planning before creative or implementation work by clarifying goals, constraints, options, and design details.
skill.summary: Turn early ideas into validated designs through structured dialogue.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and shell execution patterns, but the reviewed file contains only Markdown instructions. The cryptography alerts are prose false positives, and the backtick alert is a Markdown path reference. The skill does direct agents to write a design document and commit it to git, so publication is acceptable with low-risk filesystem and git-operation awareness.

low_findings.title: Filesystem and Git Workflow Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to write a validated design to docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md and commit the document to git. This is a normal planning workflow, but it can modify the user's repository if followed without confirmation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs document creation and a git commit. The context is benign documentation workflow guidance, with no evidence of hidden commands, obfuscation, network access, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography findings occur in ordinary English planning text, headings, and documentation instructions. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher, or password handling code appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content is Markdown guidance about brainstorming and design validation. There is no executable code or security-sensitive cryptographic operation at the reported lines.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Alert Is a Markdown False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution pattern is a Markdown inline-code path, not executable Ruby, shell, or script content. The file does not define a command runner or pass user input to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 38 uses backticks only to format a destination file path in Markdown. The surrounding file contains no interpreter context where those backticks would execute.

content.value_statement: Early product and engineering ideas often fail because requirements stay vague. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through focused questions, options, and a validated design before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Reviews current project context before planning when files, docs, or commits are available.
Asks one focused question at a time to clarify purpose, constraints, and success criteria.
Compares two or three possible approaches with trade-offs and a recommendation.
Presents design sections in manageable 200 to 300 word chunks for validation.
Covers architecture, components, data flow, error handling, and testing in the design.
Can guide creation of a dated design document after the plan is validated.
limitations: It does not implement the design by itself.
It depends on the user to answer clarification questions.
It may need repository access to review current files, docs, or recent commits.
Git commits and document writes should be confirmed before execution.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Feature
use_cases.description: Clarify user goals, compare possible feature shapes, and produce a design that is ready for engineering review.

use_cases.title: Design a Technical Change
use_cases.description: Explore architecture, data flow, error handling, and tests before changing an existing codebase.

use_cases.title: Turn Ambiguous Requests Into Specs
use_cases.description: Use structured questions to transform a rough stakeholder request into a validated implementation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Brainstorm
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin planning when the idea is still rough.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to help me shape this idea: [describe the idea]. Ask one question at a time and help me define the goal.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Implementation Approaches
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose between several possible ways to build something.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to explore 2 to 3 approaches for [feature or change]. Include trade-offs, risks, and your recommended option.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Validated Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a design before implementation starts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to turn [idea] into a design. Cover architecture, components, data flow, error handling, and testing. Validate each section with me.

prompt_templates.title: Plan From Existing Repository Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Design a change inside an existing project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to inspect the current project context, ask focused questions, compare options, and produce a design document plan for [change].

output_examples.input: I want to add team-level notifications to our app.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks who receives notifications first, then compares event-based, digest-based, and rule-based approaches with trade-offs.

output_examples.input: We need to redesign onboarding but the requirements are unclear.
output_examples.output: The assistant narrows the goal, identifies success criteria, and presents a staged onboarding design for review in short sections.

output_examples.input: Help me plan a safer data import workflow.
output_examples.output: The assistant explores validation, preview, error recovery, and testing choices before recommending a design path.

best_practices: Answer each clarification question with concrete constraints and success criteria.
Review each design section before moving to the next one.
Confirm file writes and git commits before allowing repository changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to skip requirements discovery and start coding immediately.
Do not accept the first proposed approach without comparing alternatives.
Do not let it commit design documents without checking the content first.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps turn early ideas into structured designs through questions, options, and staged validation.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on planning and design before implementation begins.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing repository?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review project files, docs, and recent commits when those are available.

faq.question: Why does it ask one question at a time?
faq.answer: One question keeps the discussion focused and reduces unclear or incomplete answers.

faq.question: Does it create documents?
faq.answer: It can guide writing a validated design document under docs/plans after the user approves the design.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains Markdown guidance only, with low-risk repository write and git workflow instructions.
